params.name: charge
params.description: Only return disputes associated to the charge specified by this charge ID.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: payment_intent
params.description: Only return disputes associated to the PaymentIntent specified by this PaymentIntent ID.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves the dispute with the given ID.

When you get a dispute, contacting your customer is always the best first step. If that doesn’t work, you can submit evidence to help us resolve the dispute in your favor. You can do this in your dashboard, but if you prefer, you can use the API to submit evidence programmatically.

Depending on your dispute type, different evidence fields will give you a better chance of winning your dispute. To figure out which evidence fields to pr

Closing the dispute for a charge indicates that you do not have any evidence to submit and are essentially dismissing the dispute, acknowledging it as lost.

The status of the dispute will change from needs_response to lost. Closing a dispute is irreversible.
